questions.question: Explain five main services provided by an Operating System to user programs.
keyPoints: Program Execution - loading and running programs
I/O Operations - handling input/output devices
File System Manipulation - creating, reading, writing files
Error Detection - detecting and handling errors
Resource Allocation - allocating CPU, memory, I/O resources

questions.question: What are System Calls? Explain how a system call is executed in an operating system.
keyPoints: System calls are programming interface to OS kernel services
User program calls library function (API)
System call number placed in register
Trap/interrupt instruction executed
CPU switches from user mode to kernel mode
Kernel executes the service and returns control

questions.question: Differentiate between User Mode and Kernel Mode in operating systems.
keyPoints: User Mode: Limited privileges, cannot execute privileged instructions
Kernel Mode: Full privileges, complete hardware access
User programs run in user mode
OS kernel runs in kernel mode
Context switch occurs via trap/interrupt

questions.question: List and briefly explain four categories of system calls.
keyPoints: Process Control: fork(), exec(), wait(), exit()
File Management: open(), read(), write(), close()
Device Management: ioctl(), read(), write()
Information Maintenance: getpid(), gettime(), date()
Communication: pipe(), socket(), msgget()
Protection: chmod(), chown(), umask()

questions.question: What is the difference between an API and a System Call? Give an example.
keyPoints: API: Set of function specifications available to applications
System Call: Actual invocation of OS kernel service
API may map to one or more system calls
Example: printf() API may use write() system call
POSIX and WIN32 are examples of APIs

questions.question: Explain the purpose of process control system calls with examples.
keyPoints: fork(): Creates new child process
exec(): Replaces process image with new program
wait(): Parent waits for child termination
exit(): Terminates calling process
kill(): Sends signal to process

questions.question: Describe the operations performed by file management system calls.
keyPoints: open(): Opens file and returns file descriptor
read(): Reads data from file
write(): Writes data to file
close(): Closes open file descriptor
lseek(): Repositions file pointer
unlink(): Deletes file from file system

questions.question: Explain the System Call Interface in detail. Describe the sequence of events that occurs when a user program makes a system call.
keyPoints: System call interface acts as bridge between user space and kernel space
User program invokes library function (API wrapper)
API places system call number in designated register
Parameters placed in registers or memory
Trap instruction (software interrupt) executed
CPU switches from user mode to kernel mode
Kernel identifies system call number from register
Kernel executes appropriate service routine
Results placed in return register
Control returns to user mode via iret instruction
User program receives return value

questions.question: Discuss in detail the various types of system calls provided by an operating system. Give at least two examples of each type.
keyPoints: Process Control: fork, exec, wait, exit, getpid, kill
File Management: open, read, write, close, lseek, chmod
Device Management: ioctl, read, write, open, close
Information Maintenance: getpid, gettime, date, uname, sysinfo
Communication: pipe, msgget, shmget, socket, bind, connect
Protection: chmod, chown, umask, setuid, getuid

questions.question: What are the main services of an Operating System? Explain each service in detail with its significance.
keyPoints: Program Execution: Loading programs into memory and executing them
I/O Operations: Managing input/output devices efficiently
File System Manipulation: Creating, organizing, reading, writing files
Error Detection: Detecting hardware and software errors
Resource Allocation: Managing CPU, memory, and I/O resources among processes
Protection and Security: Ensuring controlled access to system resources
Communication: Enabling inter-process communication
Accounting: Tracking system resource usage
